why_it_matters: Atenolol is renally cleared and can accumulate to dangerous levels with GFR 11, and its beta-blockade likely explains the admission bradycardia (HR 57), making omission from med reconciliation potentially harmful.
Documenting serious-prognosis counseling given advanced age and comorbidities is essential for goals-of-care clarity and completeness of the family communication record.
The SpO2 response to oxygen (improvement to low 90s) documents effectiveness of the intervention and informs ongoing titration and escalation decisions.
